meaning: adj.不调和的,不和的,[乐]不悦耳的,不和谐的
examples: Something gave a loud discordant twang.
His agenda is discordant with ours.
Remember a discordant mind will have great difficulty merging with nature.
The Sarkozy-Bush mood music could not be more positive, but there are a few discordant notes.
But she had the same ringlets bouncing around her ears, the same sweetly discordant face.
